# Week 3: Standard Algorithms

## Video 3

### Simon's Algorithm

- [Music] hello everyone i am dhiraj and today we are going to learn about grover search algorithm so the problem setting is as follows that we are given a large database which consists of capital n elements and the goal is to search for a single element so let's say that we have our elements which are labeled a1 up to a capital n so you can think of this database as arranged in some form of an array or a list and now you need to search for a given element so the question is does there exist an x such that a sub x equals y and you need to find this x if it exists so i would like you to think about how much time would you take to search for a given element given the given unordered array or list of such elements so you would have figured out that it will take order n time and the way to do it is to iterate through each element sequentially and then you will be able to uh just keep check and then you will just be able to check whether there is some element uh a ax which equals y or not and we can't do better than linear time right so you will have to look at every element at least once so this is the best possible classically the question now is can we do better and the answer is that yes we can do better and for this we have and basically in in quantum setting we can do better and to do so we need grover search algorithm which is the topic of today's class so to be able to formally define global search algorithm we need the idea of something called an oracle which you might have seen which you might also have seen in the previous lectures but ah let us revise let's revisit that so so formally an oracle a classical oracle to be more specific is some function f which which in our case it it takes an input from the set of size n and returns either 0 or 1 for every input so basically for all x from 1 to n f x is either 0 or 1 and the goal now in the setting is to find an x such that f x equals 1 so basically we have modeled our problem through an oracle so for example for this database kind of setting the oracle will uh the oracle will return uh so so here f x will be equal to 1 if ax equals y so for the given y we will have such an oracle right but ah you can think of this oracle setting as more general and the elements need not come from some specific database they can indeed come from uh you can actually create uh different type of articles for example even even in cryptography you have this concept of random oracles so you just have some functions and the functions look truly random to you okay so so but but this database is a motivating example database search is a motivating example so now uh so so basically here in our case we have this oracle which takes x as input and it returns fxs output so how does this oracle look in quantum world in quantum as we know we are used to this idea of qubits and secondly we also want the computation to be reversible so the way to look at an oracle in quantum setting is as follows that the oracle takes two registers the first is an input register so for example for if if the input is of ah represents elements from 1 to n then this input registers will consist of log n qubits and then there is an output register when oracle acts on these on this pair of registers it keeps the first register as it is and to the second register which is essentially it was a single qubit register it exhausts it with fx so if i say if i send a 0 in the second register i am just going to get the value of f x in the second register right so if i have of x on 0 i am going to get x and f x but ok so so but now this is something more general so i could have also sent a one in the second register this would have given me x and one xor f x and we know that 1 x or f x is nothing but f x complement now the magic happens so what if i send a superposition of 0 and 1 in our case we are going to send minus minus 8 which as you know is nothing but 0 minus 1 over root 2 this is the minus state so what happens here so here this

oracle acts on the superposition and this returns x f x minus x fx complement over root 2 right because when it acts on x 1 we have seen that it returns x and x x and f x complement when it acts on x 0 it returns x and f x and this this since ah this is the linear operator i am just going to take the corresponding linear combination okay now we can actually now simplify this right so let's take cases so case one is when f x is zero in this case o f acting on x and minus is going to be x 0 minus x 1 right complement of 0 is 1 over root 2 which is just x and minus and now we take the alternate case when f x was 1 here when o f acts on x and minus this gives us x 1 minus x 0 over root 2 which indeed turns out to be minus of x and minus 8. right so in either case we see that of when it acts on x and minus it gives me minus 1 to the power of f x x and minus right so this is for how we can write it in general right this will be true for both cases okay so now how do we design a quantum algorithm based on this oracle that is the question okay so okay so just to remark that this is now known as a phase oracle in the sense that the oracle now acts by applying a phase on the state of the input qubit right so if if the input qubit at x there is a additional phase of minus 1 to the power of f x which gets multiplied when i apply this oracle so now we need to see that how do we use this phase oracle to implement our grover search algorithm so firstly the the additional advantage which we have in using such an oracle is that this allows us to query in superposition meaning that since it's a quantum particle not just i can query on standard basis states x i can even query it on a superposition of states right and ah so how do we do that and so so let's okay so the easiest superposition let's let's start with something which is the superposition of all states so we start with the state psi naught which which is going to be the uniform superposition of all states and this is defined as 1 over root n sum over x get x right so i have the superposition of all states over here and now let's uh split it up into two parts so there is one part which is uh and let's let's assume that the element a is the one which is which which we are searching for okay so basically let's assume that assume that f of a is 1 okay and f of x is 0 for all x not equal to a so here we are assuming that there is exactly one element which is marked by the oracle there are the other elements are not marked and we are searching for this marked element right so we are making an assumption that exactly one such element is uh marked by the oracle and this is the element which we need to search for so since this is special so let's rewrite our state by separating by segregating out this specific element so i have a plus sum over x naught equal to a and x now i want to write it as a linear combination of two specific states so i first single out the state a and the associated coefficient is one over root n and then i have the second sec uh the the other part in psi naught which is the sum over all x naught equal to a x right and uh now in order to uh in order to uh make it make it unit non i am also going to divide it by square root of n minus 1 that will make this a unit norm vector and since i have divided i will also multiply it by square root of n minus 1 and there is 1 over root n which is outside so this state is something i'll call as the state e so this state is defined to be right so essentially if we plot this this is how it looks like i have my state e and i have my state a and then i have psi naught which i have started with psi naught or u which is the uniform superposition of all states and it makes some angle with uh e right so it so firstly it lies in the span of e and a so it lies in the same plane as e and a and it makes some angle with e this angle is indeed uh so so we will have to find out what this angle is let me actually call it theta naught so this angle theta naught now satisfies the

it is going to be minus of alpha a plus beta e in other words the component of a is going to get reversed or this acts as a reflection a reflection about e in span of e and e so this oracle is a s theta and let's say that i first reflect about you and i reflect about v ah now i'll i would want you to think about what is going to happen so suppose if i take psi and let me perform a reflection actually the following lemma that ah two successive reflections in a plane lead to a rotation by twice theta where theta is the angle between the consecutive reflections consecutive reflecting lines
